t_ch: 啊…姐姐……
t_en: Ah... Big Sis...

t_ch: 您…难道抛下我了吗…？
t_en: Have you... abandoned me...?

t_ch: 啊…有人在那里吗？在窗户外面……
t_en: Ah... is someone there? Outside the window...

t_ch: 我的眼睛…看不见东西。还请您原谅我的失礼……
t_en: My eyes... I can't see. Please forgive my rudeness...

t_ch: 陌生人…请问您可曾见过一位女性？她的声音温柔，还戴着饰有铃铛的发带……
t_en: Stranger... have you seen a girl around? Her voice is soft and kind, and she wears a headband with a bell...

t_ch: 那铃铛会发出清脆的响声，好听极了……
t_en: The bell chimes with crisp clarity. It's a joy to hear...

t_ch: 那人…是我的姐姐。她已出门许久，却没有回到家中……
t_en: That person... is my elder sister. She has been out for a while and hasn't come home yet...

t_ch: 姐姐对我说，她要去的地方叫作…「裂界」。在那儿能够寻得值钱的东西……
t_en: She told me she was going to some place called... the "Fragmentum," and she could find something worth selling there...

t_ch: 若您曾见过我的姐姐，可否转告她速归…？
t_en: If you've seen my sister, could you help me tell her to come home quickly...?

t_ch: 她的妹妹十分孤单，正在家中等她……
t_en: Her younger sister is waiting for her all alone at home...

t_ch: 你对这个有印象吗？
t_en: Does this ring any bells?

t_ch: 啊啊…是铃铛声…！
t_en: Ahh... the sound of the bell...!

t_ch: 那熟悉的、清脆的响声…姐姐，是您回来了吗……
t_en: That familiar, crisp chime... Big Sis, are you back...

t_ch: 啊…这不祥的气味……
t_en: Ah... an ominous air...

t_ch: 啊…啊啊……
t_en: Ah... agghh...

t_ch: 不…不…怎么会这样……
t_en: No... No... How could this be...
Ah...

t_ch: 姐姐…姐姐……
t_en: Big Sis... Big Sis...

t_ch: 您…终于丢下我了吗……
t_en: Have you... finally abandoned me...

t_ch: 虚弱的啜泣声渐渐微弱。
t_en: The already-weak sobbing fades away gradually.

t_ch: 没有见过你的姐姐。
t_en: I have not seen your big sister.

t_ch: 啊…我明白了。谢谢您，好心的陌生人……
t_en: Ah... I understand. Thank you, kind stranger...

t_ch: 姐姐啊…您到底去了哪里…？
t_en: Big Sis... where on earth have you gone...?

t_ch: 窗后的暖光依旧如薄暮般昏暗。
t_en: The warm light behind the window is still dim as dusk.

t_ch: 你试图敲打窗棂求取回应，却只得到一片死寂。
t_en: You try rapping on the window for a response, only to be greeted by a deathly silence.

t_ch: 准备离开时，你在窗台下的阴影中发现了一只小巧的八音盒。
t_en: When you try to leave, you notice an exquisite music box by the shadow of the window sill.

t_ch: 转动滞涩的发条，你听到几近崩溃的机械结构相互倾轧，发出令人不安的乐声。
t_en: Creaky springs turn, and you hear the sequential jostling of tired mechanical switches and cogs sing an eerie melody.

t_ch: 你去哪儿了…？
t_en: Where have you been...?

t_ch: 噢、噢…！路过的好心人啊，求求你，请帮帮我……
t_en: Oh, oh...! Kind passerby, please, please help me...

t_ch: 我的妹妹…她离家出走了！我四处寻找，却怎么也找不到……
t_en: My little sister... she ran away from home! I've looked everywhere for her, but I can't find her...

t_ch: 她的身体虚弱，眼睛也看不见东西…要是一个人在外面出事了，我该如何是好？
t_en: She's always been frail, and her eyes can't see... whatever would I do, if something happened to her out there?

t_ch: 啊，我真是个不合格的姐姐…竟会让这种事情发生…！
t_en: Argh, I'm a failure of a big sister... to have let such a thing happen...!

t_ch: 在你眼前的这位女性，看起来十分焦虑。
t_en: The girl before you appears very anxious.

t_ch: 你对这个有印象吗？
t_en: Does this ring any bells?

t_ch: …这是我们的八音盒……
t_en: ...This is our music box...

t_ch: 你听，我的好妹妹……
t_en: Listen, my darling sister...

t_ch: 多么…悦耳……
t_en: How... pleasing...

t_ch: 欢迎……
t_en: Welcome...

t_ch: 回…家……
t_en: Go... home...

t_ch: 她那憔悴的面庞上浮现出一丝安心的笑容。
t_en: A faint smile of comfort lines her drained visage.

t_ch: 片刻，那笑容倏然消散——同她本人一起于裂界中溶解。
t_en: In an instant, that smile fades and dissolves — along with her person — into the Fragmentum.

t_ch: ——裂界中的又一个回音残响彻底消失了。
t_en: ...Another echo of the Cadence lost in the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 我没有见过你的妹妹。
t_en: I have not seen your little sister before.

t_ch: 啊…我究竟该怎么办才好……
t_en: Ah... whatever shall I do...

t_ch: 喂，你！
t_en: Hey, you!

t_ch: 你这家伙——终于让我抓到你了！
t_en: I got you now!

t_ch: 您哪位？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: …我是谁，这重要吗？告诉你，我是正直的贝洛伯格人民公仆，荣誉的银鬃铁卫列兵！
t_en: ...I am an honorable Silvermane Guard and a righteous servant to the people of Belobog!

t_ch: 肆意破坏公物的家伙，今天被我抓了个现行！不给你点颜色瞧瞧，简直妄对我的铁卫誓词！
t_en: I caught you redhanded, you vandal! Under my sworn oath as a Silvermane Guard, I shall carry out proper justice!

t_ch: 我、我投降…
t_en: I... I give up...

t_ch: 什么？！你连自己犯了什么事都不知道，怎么一秒就怂了？
t_en: What? Did you just give up without knowing what you did?

t_ch: 不行！肆意破坏公物的家伙，今天不好好教训教训你，简直妄对我的铁卫誓词！
t_en: You won't get off that easy, vandal! You will taste the iron fist of justice!

t_ch: 我只是想恢复一下状态…
t_en: I'm just trying to stay in shape...

t_ch: 好家伙，你还有理了？
t_en: Are you trying to reason with me?

t_ch: 我告诉你——你每打碎一件补给容器，禁区的某位铁卫兄弟就得多加班三个小时，把新的容器放到原处……
t_en: You should know, for every supply vessel you break, that's three hours of overtime for someone in the restricted zone to replace it...

t_ch: 这是我的自由，你管不着！
t_en: What I do is my right and it's none of your business!

t_ch: 还在嘴硬？看来必须得让你尝点苦头才行了——
t_en: Backtalking, are you? Guess I got to put some hurting on you...

t_ch: 今天弟兄们就要让你知道，破坏禁区公物要付出什么代价！给我上！
t_en: We'll show you the price to pay for damaging public property in the restricted zone! Get 'em!

t_ch: 对不起，我知道错了。
t_en: I'm sorry. I was wrong.

t_ch: 呃…道歉要是有用，还要银鬃铁卫干嘛？你…呃…我必须让你知道事情的严重性！对，严重性！
t_en: If apologies worked, we wouldn't need Silvermane Guards! I must show you how serious this is. Yeah, how serious!

t_ch: 摊牌了！我就是公物破坏狂！
t_en: Fine. I admit I'm a repeat offender.

t_ch: 我就知道，你的歉意都是装出来的！
t_en: I knew all that apologizing was an act!

t_ch: 今天弟兄们就要让你知道，破坏禁区公物要付出什么代价！给我上！
t_en: We'll show you the price to pay for damaging public property in the restricted zone! Get 'em!

t_ch: 我真的知错了！我再也不敢了。
t_en: Please stop! I won't do it again!

t_ch: 呃…我……
t_en: Uh, I...

t_ch: …咳，我第一次碰见道歉态度这么诚恳的家伙…看来你还算明辨是非。那这次…这次就…呃…放过你吧。
t_en: ...Ahem, you're the first one to apologize so sincerely. I guess I, uh... can let you go this time.

t_ch: …但是，但是！你得给我时刻记住——破坏公物者，虽远必诛！
t_en: ...But know this — no vandals will be tolerated!

t_ch: 行了，快走吧。禁区本来就不是任你游荡的地方。
t_en: All right, you may leave now. The restricted zone is not a hangout.

t_ch: 这玩意不是会自动刷新吗？
t_en: Don't these things renew themselves?

t_ch: 自动刷新？你怕不是在做梦吧！
t_en: Renew? Are you from another world?

t_ch: 我告诉你——你每打碎一件补给容器，禁区的某位铁卫兄弟就得多加班三个小时，把新的容器放到原处……
t_en: You should know, for every supply vessel you break, that's three hours of overtime for someone in the restricted zone to replace it...

t_en: What I do is my right and it's none of your business!

t_en: Backtalking, are you? Guess I got to put some hurting on you...

t_en: We'll show you the price to pay for damaging public property in the restricted zone! Get 'em!

t_en: I'm sorry. I was wrong.

t_en: We'll show you the price to pay for damaging public property in the restricted zone! Get 'em!

t_en: Please stop! I won't do it again!

t_ch: 呃…我……
t_en: Uh, I...

t_ch: 对不起，我知道错了。
t_en: I'm sorry. I was wrong.

t_ch: 呃…光道歉有什么用？
t_en: You think an apology is enough?

t_ch: 我告诉你——你每打碎一件补给容器，禁区的某位铁卫兄弟就得多加班三个小时，把新的容器放到原处……
t_en: You should know, for every supply vessel you break, that's three hours of overtime for someone in the restricted zone to replace it...

t_ch: 虽然但是…这关我什么事？
t_en: But... what's that got to do with me?

t_ch: 不、不关你事？你知道银鬃铁卫的休息时间有多宝贵吗？你知道浪费别人三个小时的生命是多可耻的一件事吗？
t_en: "What's that got to do with you"? Don't you know how precious breaks are to Silvermane Guards? Don't you know how precious three hours are to another person?

t_ch: 我就知道，你之前的歉意都是装出来的！
t_en: I knew all that apologizing was an act!

t_ch: 今天弟兄们就要让你知道，破坏禁区公物要付出什么代价！给我上！
t_en: We'll show you the price to pay for damaging public property in the restricted zone! Get 'em!

t_ch: 我真的知错了！我再也不敢了。
t_en: Please stop! I won't do it again!

t_ch: 呃…我……
t_en: Uh, I...

t_ch: …咳，我第一次碰见道歉态度这么诚恳的家伙…看来你还算明辨是非。那这次…这次就…呃…放过你吧。
t_en: ...Ahem, you're the first one to apologize so sincerely. I guess I, uh... can let you go this time.

t_ch: …但是，但是！你得给我时刻记住——破坏公物者，虽远必诛！
t_en: ...But know this — no vandals will be tolerated!

t_ch: 行了，快走吧。禁区本来就不是任你游荡的地方。
t_en: All right, you may leave now. The restricted zone is not a hangout.

t_ch: 可…可恶…这家伙，有点本事！不是一般的破坏狂。
t_en: You... You're pretty tough for a vandal!

t_ch: 唔…可恶啊！居然连最基本的公物都保护不了，真是狼狈…真是愧对邓恩队长…！
t_en: I can't even protect the most basic infrastructure... I've brought shame to Captain Dunn!

t_ch: 打碎容器是为了保护和平。
t_en: I broke the vessels to preserve peace.

t_ch: 居然能面不改色心不跳地说出这种话…看来你确实不是一般人！
t_en: You got to be something else to say that with a straight face!

t_ch: 邓恩队长也打不过我。
t_en: Captain Dunn isn't a match for me, either.

t_ch: …你和邓恩队长交过手？难怪…连队长都没能战胜的对手，我一个列兵又有什么机会？是我自不量力了……
t_en: You fought Captain Dunn? No wonder... How can a soldier like me beat someone even the captain couldn't defeat? You're way above my league...

t_ch: 没人能阻止我——没有人！
t_en: No one can stop me! No one!

t_ch: 居然以破坏公物为乐…该死！我怎么会输给这种人……
t_en: How I can lose to someone who relishes in the destruction of public property?

t_ch: 唉，算了…拳头不够硬，说什么都是白搭！既然拦不住你，那就只能由你去了……
t_en: Oh, forget it. I can't beat you, so do what you will...

t_ch: 但是，你给我记住——有朝一日，等我修炼归来…我一定会为被无端破坏的补给容器们报仇！你给我等着！
t_en: But I'll be back one day to avenge the broken supply vessel. Just you wait!

t_ch: …破坏狂，给我伏法吧！
t_en: ...Bow down, vandal!

t_ch: 哈，刚才你不是很嚣张吗？怎么，这就被打服了？
t_en: Not so tough now, are you?

t_ch: 这次就当是一次警告——以后你再路过补给容器的时候，最好给我多长个心眼！
t_en: Take this as a warning to never mess with another supply vessel again!

t_ch: 好了，赶紧从我眼前消失…铁卫禁区不欢迎你这种家伙。
t_en: Now remove yourself from my sight! The Silvermane Guards don't want vandals like you in the restricted zone!

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 这是一只造型古典的皮质手提箱，表面落了一层厚厚的灰尘。旅行箱会出现在车站，可以说是再正常不过…但它属于正常状况中相对异常的那种。
t_en: This leather suitcase has a vintage design, with layer upon layer of dust piling up on top of it. Typically, seeing such luggage at a transportation station is nothing unusual. So it can be said that this suitcase's lonely presence still falls within the realm of what is considered normal, even though it is still somewhat out of the ordinary.

t_ch: 毕竟没有旅行者会把自己心爱的行李箱丢在路边——那么久——如果真有，想必那人一定经常丢三落四，或者至少…很有钱。
t_en: After all, no traveler would ever throw away their prized luggage on the roadside like that, at least, not for that long. And if this were somehow the case, then the person who left their stuff behind must be habitually forgetful or unbelievably rich.

t_ch: 而且，这位迷糊或富有的旅行者还在自己的提箱里留下了某种不得了的物件。如果伛身侧耳仔细倾听，你便能听到这样一种声音……
t_en: Moreover, this forgetful or rich traveler apparently left something quite extraordinary inside this suitcase. If you press your ears against it and have a listen, you can hear a sound that goes like this...

t_ch: 滴答，滴答。
t_en: Tick tock, tick tock.

t_ch: 滴答，滴答。
t_en: Tick tock, tick tock.

t_ch: 这似乎是某种机械装置读秒的声音。令人沉静，但出现在这里可不太妙——人来人往的车站、会发出滴答声的可疑提箱……
t_en: It appears to be some kind of mechanized device knocking a sound with each passing second, calm, soothing. But, wait... Having this thing here is probably problematic. This suspicious suitcase making ticking sounds in a transportation station this busy...

t_ch: 天啊，太可怕了，还是别往下继续说了。呃，你懂这是什么意思吧？相信你一定懂。
t_en: Gosh, that's terrible. Stop right there. Uh, you know what that means, right? I'm sure you know.

t_ch: 我懂——快来人啊！有炸弹！
t_en: I know... Call someone, please! There's a bomb here!

t_ch: 停、停，你先别急！会发出这种声音的也未必只有炸弹——它还有可能是块价值连城的金表呢，对不对？在没有得到定论之前，你最好不要引发恐慌。
t_en: Shh! Just hold on for a sec! It's not just bombs that could be ticking. What about precious gold watches? You'd better not start a mass panic before a conclusion is reached.

t_ch: 幸运的是，你未经思考的呼喊没有引起任何人的注意；不幸的是，你不知道这箱子里滴答响的东西究竟是炸弹还是金表。
t_en: Fortunately, your reckless yelling didn't cause anyone to notice us. And unfortunately, we still don't know if it's a bomb or just some kind of timepiece.

t_ch: 我懂——或许是块昂贵的金表…
t_en: I know... Maybe it's a valuable gold watch.

t_ch: 停、停，你先别急！会发出这种声音的也未必只有钟表——它还有可能是颗危险的炸弹呢，对不对？歪脑筋很有可能会要你的命，下次还是别这样了。
t_en: Wait, wait, just hold on for a sec! It's not just watches that could be ticking. What about bombs and explosive devices? We'd better be sure of it lest it kills us all.

t_ch: 幸运的是，你的手比大脑更冷静，它暂时还未伸向那诱人的皮箱；不幸的是，你不知道这箱子里滴答响的东西究竟是炸弹还是金表。
t_en: Fortunately, you are calmer than what your words suggest, and you haven't reached into that alluring suitcase. And unfortunately, we still don't have a single clue about whether it's a bomb or just some kind of timepiece.

t_ch: 我不懂。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 噢，老实说，你的回答十分让人出乎意料。
t_en: Well, to be honest, your answer was quite unexpected.

t_ch: 我是想说，这箱子里很有可能揣着一颗炸弹…或是一块价值连城的金表。不过，后者发生的概率比较小…也许。
t_en: What I'm trying to say is it could be a bomb in the suitcase... or a precious gold watch. But the possibility for the latter is rather slim... I think.

t_ch: 无论如何，你得打开这个箱子才能知道里面装了什么。但这听起来有点…不道德。
t_en: Either way, we've got to open the suitcase to know what is inside. But that sounds a little... dishonest.

t_ch: 换句话说——要想做出这种违反公序良俗的事，你就必须消耗一个「崇高道德的赞许」，以及忍受自己良心的拷问、周围人惊异的目光、银鬃铁卫的通缉…诸如此类。
t_en: In other words, to do something that goes against societal ethics would cost you a "Praise of High Morals," not to mention the guilt you'd have to endure, the look you'd get from others, the warrant for your arrest by the Silvermane Guards, and so on.

t_ch: 或者，你就干脆闭上眼、转过身，默默离开这里，当作今天在这里目击到的一切根本不存在——啊，当然还得祈祷那颗可能存在的炸弹不会危害公共安全。
t_en: Or, you could just turn a blind eye to all this, turn around, and leave this place, pretending everything you just witnessed has never even happened in the first place. But, of course, you will still have to pray that this potential bomb is not going to risk public safety.

t_ch: 我必须打开这个箱子！
t_en: I have to open this suitcase!

t_ch: 没问题！那么，你曾受过「崇高道德的赞许」吗？
t_en: No problem! So, were you ever blessed with "Praise of High Morals"?

t_ch: 我当然有。
t_en: Of course, I have.

t_ch: 如果崇高道德确有实体，那么它大概会在此刻向你皱眉。
t_en: If High Morals were a real person, it'd probably be frowning so hard at you right now.

t_ch: 但一码归一码——消耗「崇高道德的赞许」后，你就可以心安理得地打开这可疑的箱子了。上吧！
t_en: But one thing at a time, you will have nothing to worry about when you consume a "Praise of High Morals" to open this suspicious suitcase. So, go ahead!

t_ch: 都什么时候了还谈这个？
t_en: We don't have time for this!

t_ch: 嘿，这是规矩。你懂什么是规矩，我也懂。
t_en: Heh, you know them as well as I do that these are the rules.

t_ch: 没有那个镀金的、健美的、昂扬的大拇指人像，你就不能做坏事！明白吗？
t_en: Without that proud-looking, gold-plated, thumb-sized bodybuilder figurine, you can't do anything bad! Got it?

t_ch: 好吧，那我就用一个。
t_en: Well, then, I will use one.

t_ch: 这就对了——规矩不能坏嘛。消耗「崇高道德的赞许」后，你就可以心安理得地打开这可疑的箱子了。上吧！
t_en: That's it. Rules are made to be followed. You will have nothing to worry about when you use a "Praise of High Morals" to open this suspicious suitcase. So, go ahead!

t_ch: 那可是个炸弹啊！
t_en: But that's an actual bomb!

t_ch: 你很急，这可以理解；但擅自打开别人的行李箱就是窥探他人隐私！这是不对的！
t_en: I know you are in a hurry. But opening others' suitcases without their explicit permission is an invasion of privacy! This is simply wrong!

t_ch: 哎，那还是用吧。
t_en: *sigh* I will use it.

t_ch: 你果然是个正直的人。消耗「崇高道德的赞许」后，你就可以心安理得地打开这可疑的箱子了。上吧！
t_en: I knew you are a person of integrity. You will have nothing to worry about when you use a "Praise of High Morals" to open this suspicious suitcase. So, go ahead!

t_ch: 那！是！个！炸！弹！
t_en: That! Is! A! Bomb!

t_ch: 喔喔喔——冷静！有什么需求可以坐下来好好谈嘛，别激动！
t_en: Whoa whoa whoa, take it easy! Let's just sit down and have a chat. No need to get all riled up.

t_ch: 算了，算了！你请便，就打开它看看吧——反正没人知道这是谁的箱子……
t_en: Fine, you go ahead and open it. No one knows whose suitcase this is anyway.

t_ch: 你打开了这个箱子……
t_en: You open this suitcase...

t_ch: 你终于在这空荡荡的手提箱中发现了滴答声的来源——这果然是一只价值连城的金表。
t_en: You finally locate the source of that ticking sound in the rather empty suitcase. It turns out to be a precious gold watch!

t_ch: 上面刻有「华劳斯」的字样，另附一行「如有好心人拾到此物，请速联系华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，必有重谢」。
t_en: The moniker "Wallace" is engraved on the watch, along with the line, "Should this item come into your possession, kindly contact Wallace Cambridge Limestein. Your act of kindness shall be handsomely recompensed."

t_ch: 看来，这块表会出现在这里是命中注定。这位富豪已经考虑到了所有的可能性了——除了被当成炸弹。
t_en: This watch seems destined to be here at this very moment, as its wealthy owner has already made preparations for all the foreseeable fates of this watch, with the possible exception that it might be mistaken for a bomb.

t_ch: 风险太大，我还是走吧。
t_en: It's still risky. Perhaps I should just leave.

t_ch: 那么，一桩贝洛伯格缆车站的闹剧就此落幕——
t_en: Then that will be the end of this farce at Belobog's cable car station.

t_ch: 毕竟…假设它真是一颗炸弹，也不至于就这样迟迟不爆炸，而被丢在这里吃灰了。
t_en: If it were a bomb, then why hasn't it ever exploded? All this time, it was just collecting dust.

t_ch: 咔嚓
t_en: Click

t_ch: 嘿，你谁啊？
t_en: Hey, who are you?

t_ch: 呃，不管是谁，你踩在我们基地大门上很久了！能让开不？
t_en: Um, no matter who you are, can you please just get out of the way? You've been stepping on the door to our base for far too long.

t_ch: 一阵尖锐的叫骂声从下水道中传来。
t_en: Angry curses are heard coming from the sewers below.

t_ch: 什么声音？
t_en: What's that noise?

t_ch: 什么什么声音？我，米歇尔，正和你说话呢！对，就在你脚下！
t_en: Wh—What noise? It's me, Michel, speaking to you right now! Yes, right below you!

t_ch: 井盖会说话？！
t_en: The manhole cover can talk now!?

t_ch: 什么井盖？！真没素质！我叫米歇尔，懂？
t_en: What manhole cover!? How rude! I'm Michel, get it?

t_ch: 你好，米歇尔。
t_en: Hi, Michel.

t_ch: 哈哈，你还挺有礼貌。我喜欢。
t_en: Hah, so you do have manners. I like that.

t_ch: 井盖还有名字？！
t_en: The manhole cover has a name!?

t_ch: …你听不懂我说话是吧。
t_en: Do you even understand the words coming out of my mouth?

t_ch: 可惜你把我们基地大门堵死了，要不我非拎着双节棍上去会会你。
t_en: You should thank the stars our base's entrance door is now being blocked by you. Otherwise, I would be beating your rear with my nunchucks right about now.

t_ch: 所以，{M#哥们儿}{F#姐们儿}——你能把脚挪开了吗？你要是不走开，我这基地里的警报就停不了，懂吗？
t_en: So, {M#bruh}{F#girl}, can you please move your feet away from our door? As long as you're stepping on our door, the alarm in our base won't stop ringing, get it?

t_ch: 好吧，听你的。
t_en: Fine, I'll do that.

t_ch: 哈！可以，你这{M#哥们儿}{F#姐们儿}能处。有机会请你吃太阳薄饼——加奶酪的那种。
t_en: Ha! So, this {M#dude}{F#gal} does get it after all. I'll be treating you with a nice suncake, you know, the good kind with cheese and all.

t_ch: 但今天就算了。师傅喊我有事，回见！
t_en: But not today, though. Master needs me right now. So, catch you later!

t_ch: 一阵急促的脚步声消失在下水道深处。
t_en: Hurried footsteps continue to go further down the sewer until they are completely gone.

t_ch: 我今天就赖在这儿不走了。
t_en: What if I just stay put? What can you do about it?

t_ch: 哈！成吧，反正我也拿你没办法。你最好是能在这儿一直赖着，直到我彻底失去耐心为止。
t_en: Hah! Fine, it's not like I can force you to do anything. But you'd better be prepared to stay put for a long time until I thoroughly run out of patience.

t_ch: 还好，我提前买了一堆太阳薄饼…咱就看看谁先耗得过谁——喔～双倍奶酪，真香……
t_en: Thankfully, I already bought a stack of suncakes... Let's just see who is gonna outlast the other... Mmm, extra cheese, yummy!

t_ch: 下水道里只剩下无穷无尽的吧唧嘴声在回荡。
t_en: All that can be heard down the sewer is just munching sounds.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 毫无疑问，在你面前的东西是一个衣柜。
t_en: Without a doubt, what's in front of you is a closet.

t_ch: 它是一个——仅从你的感官所能接收到的表象而言——完美的衣柜。外观、容量、触感、材质、味道…无论从哪个角度出发，你都想象不出比它更完美的衣柜了。
t_en: It is — based on all the signals coming in from your sensory organs — a perfect closet. Design, capacity, tactility, build quality, and scent... No matter how you look at it, you can't possibly think of a better closet.

t_ch: …等等，味道？味道也是能用来判断衣柜优劣的维度吗？
t_en: Wait a minute, scent? How is scent a measurement of how good a closet is?

t_ch: 管它呢，它真好闻。那不是一般的檀木或是任何自然材质的味道…而是完全人造的、源自化学的美妙清香。它真诱人…诱人到你想赶紧钻进衣柜，让这味道缠裹全身。
t_en: Doesn't matter. It smells good. And it's not an odor of typical sandalwood or any other natural materials... Rather, it is a miraculously cleansing scent that is completely artificial, a marvel of chemical engineering. It is so alluring that all you want is to get into the closet and let your whole body soak in its scent.

t_ch: 抵触这致命的诱惑。
t_en: Resist the lethal temptation.

t_ch: 你抵御住了诱惑。现在，最好离这个衣柜远点…化学的气味或许香甜，但对你的健康恐怕没什么益处。
t_en: You manage to resist the temptation. And now, you'd better stay away from the closet... As ambrosial as its chemical scent might be, it probably won't do you any good.

t_ch: 管它呢——我进来了，衣柜！
t_en: Whatever, I'm going in. Here I come, closet!

t_ch: 没错…你要进去了！
t_en: Yes... you are about to go in!

t_ch: 左脚…右脚…转身…深呼吸…见鬼，为什么要深呼吸？你想打喷嚏，但努力忍住了…站定…然后……
t_en: Left foot... Right foot... Turn around... Take a deep breath... Darn it, why a deep breath? You wanna sneeze, but manage to suppress it... Stand firm... And then...

t_ch: …潇洒地关上柜门。「啪」！
t_en: ...close the door in one smooth move. Bang!

t_ch: 你成功了——祝贺你，你现在已经与全宇宙最完美的衣柜融为一体了！
t_en: Congratulations! You've now been merged with the universe's most perfect closet as one single entity.

t_ch: 不过，老实说…你的心情还远未达到「激动」的境界。或许你还需要点时间才能参会这其中的美妙。
t_en: But honestly, your mood is far from reaching the state of "excitement." Perhaps you need more time to comprehend the subtle wonder in all this.

t_ch: 嗯…对于你的视觉感官来说，这地方似乎暗了点儿。不过，你会适应的…对吧？
t_en: Hmm... This place might seem a little too dark for you. Guess you will eventually get used to it, right?

t_ch: 哈，就是这股劲！再多等一会儿，再多适应一下这黑暗的环境……
t_en: Ha, that's the spirit! Give it more time. Adapt to this dark environment.

t_ch: 出去？你确定？不，我可不建议你那么做。
t_en: Go out? Are you sure? No, I don't think that'd be good for you.

t_ch: 相信自己，只要你再稍微坚持一会儿…一定会有比伸手不见五指的黑暗更有趣的事情发生。保准带劲。
t_en: Trust yourself. Just wait a little longer... Something more interesting than this pitch darkness will occur. It's guaranteed to be the real deal.

t_ch: 等等，你听到了吗？有个声音正在接近——咔嗒，咔嗒……
t_en: Wait, did you hear that? A sound is closing in. Click, click....

t_ch: 你得想办法改变自己消极的人生观了，伙计。不，那才不是人的脚步声，那是——
t_en: You've got to change your nihilistic views on life, pal. No, that's not the sound of human footsteps. That's...

t_ch: …再大胆点，发挥你的想象力！
t_en: Go wild. Use your imagination!

t_ch: 不，那才不是什么客房服务…那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
t_en: No, that's not room service... That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.

t_ch: 没错，没错！瞧瞧，你已经完全上道了！
t_en: Yes, yes! Now you finally get it!

t_ch: 那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
t_en: That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.

t_ch: 可不是吗？好好想想，现在——猜猜看，那是谁的脚步声？
t_en: That's exactly right! Just think about it. Now, take a guess. Whose footsteps are those?

t_ch: …再大胆点，发挥你的想象力！
t_en: Go wild. Use your imagination!

t_ch: 不，那才不是什么客房服务…那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
t_en: No, that's not room service... That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.

t_ch: 没错，没错！瞧瞧，你已经完全上道了！
t_en: Yes, yes! Now you finally get it!

t_ch: 那是寄宿在歌德宾馆里的恶魔！它沉睡在肮脏的、无人打扫的小阁楼里…专门以可怜又无辜的外来者为食。
t_en: That's the Goethe Hotel's resident devil! It hibernates in dirty unattended small attics, specifically feeding on innocent and vulnerable outsiders.

t_ch: …但这回它挑错了对手！你可是宇宙闻名的开拓者，黑塔空间站的守护神，末日兽的…呃…末日。对——今天就是你为民除害的日子！
t_en: It picked the wrong opponent to mess with this time. After all, you are the universally renowned Trailblazer, the guardian deity of the Herta Space Station, the Doomsday Beast's... um... Doom. Yep. Today is the day you purge evil for the greater good!

t_ch: 衣柜里似乎没那么暗了，勇气的光芒照亮了你周围的空间。哇哦。
t_en: It doesn't seem to be that dark in the closet. The light of valor illuminates your surroundings. Wow.

t_ch: 客房服务…请问有人吗？
t_en: Room service... Is anyone in there?

t_ch: 哈，正中下怀！所有的旅馆恶魔都喜欢把自己伪装成「客房服务」。毫无新意。
t_en: Ha, there it is! All hotel devils love to disguise themselves as "room service." Such a cliche.

t_ch: 你才不会轻易被它欺骗——你的内心防线比克里珀堆砌的城墙还坚不可摧。你做好准备了。
t_en: You won't be easily fooled by it. Your mind is stronger than those city walls built by Qlipoth. You are prepared.

t_ch: 唔…我进来了哦？
t_en: Um... I'm gonna come in now, okay?

t_ch: 时机到了，恶魔——迎接正义的审判吧！
t_en: Now, devil, meet your judgment!

t_ch: 啊！啊啊啊！！有鬼，有鬼啊！！
t_en: Ah! Ahhhh! What the heck!

t_ch: 你胜利了！歌德宾馆的恶魔已经被驱逐，这片土地已经得到了净化——
t_en: You won! The Goethe Hotel's devil is now banished, and this land is now purified.

t_ch: 呃…那个「恶魔」似乎丢下了什么东西。拿着吧，别客气——就把它当作对你英勇行径的奖赏。
t_en: Um... That devil seems to have left something behind. Take it as your reward for your intrepidity.

t_ch: 只是有人在走廊里走动而已。
t_en: It's just someone walking in the corridor.

t_ch: 客房服务。
t_en: Room service.

t_ch: 歌德宾馆的恶魔……
t_en: The Goethe Hotel's devil...

t_ch: 带劲的事要来了？
t_en: The real deal is coming?

t_ch: 「嘭」的一声打开柜门——
t_en: Bang! The closet's door slams open.

t_ch: ——「受死吧，宾馆恶魔！」
t_en: "Die! You hotel devil!"

t_ch: 黑夜给了我黑色的眼睛…
t_en: Dark night gifted me a set of dark eyes...

t_ch: 我要出去。
t_en: I want out.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的人物毛色灰白、雌雄莫辨，直视着你的眸子中流露出一股若即若离的生无可恋感。
t_en: There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a wanted poster. The figure in the picture is predominantly grayish-white, androgynous, and looking straight at you with a sense of lukewarm lifelessness in their eyes.

t_ch: 再加上那似乎是手的结构连接着一根似乎是球棍的大棒——画面中的人物究竟是谁已不言而喻。
t_en: Coupled with a structure that appears to be a hand gripping a baseball bat, it's instantly clear to whom the figure refers.

t_ch: 你决定将它从墙上揭下。为了贝洛伯格，也为了你自己。
t_en: You decide to peel it off the wall. For the sake of Belobog — and for your own sake.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的人物长得很是别致——两只眼睛一个鼻子一张嘴；如果描述得再深入一点——他的头发是蓝的，眼睛是绿的。
t_en: There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The figure is highly unique: Two eyes, a nose, and a mouth; or to be more descriptive, his deep blue hair and jade-green eyes.

t_ch: 这不是在敷衍你，而是画师将人物刻画得太过传神，以至于此处再有任何描述都是画蛇添足。你看，他那似笑非笑的嘲讽神情已经能够说明一切了。
t_en: This isn't going over your head, but the portrait really is so expressive that any description here would be gilding the lily. Look, his half-smiling sneer says it all, really.

t_ch: 你决定将它从墙上揭下。主要是为了贝洛伯格——考虑到你在此地经历的种种，你并不是很在乎画中人物的感受。
t_en: You decide to peel it off the wall. Mainly for Belobog — thinking of all you've been through here, you don't really care how the character in the poster feels.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。不过，画中的人物眉宇间透露着几分沉静，又带有些许威慑，倒不似画作本身那样狂野。
t_en: There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. But this guy's eyebrows betray a hint of serenity, as well as slight gravitas — yet not as wild as the poster itself.

t_ch: 你基本上能够确认这位正与你面面相觑的男子是谁了。如果你将画像带回列车交给那人过目，他恐怕会露出与画中完全一致的表情。
t_en: You can basically confirm who this guy looking down at you is. If you take the poster back to the train and show it to that person, he'll probably show exactly the same expression as is in the poster.

t_ch: 你决定将它从墙上揭下。为了贝洛伯格，也为了你的同伴。
t_en: You decide to peel it off the wall. For the sake of Belobog — and for the sake of your companion.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 此处贴着一幅狂放不羁的画像…或者说，通缉令。不得不说，此画艺术造诣之高堪称…骇目惊心。
t_en: There's an expressively unbridled portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. It has to be said that the aesthetics of this artist are... shocking to the eye.

t_ch: 狂乱的笔触与大胆的配色相结合，将画中人物勾勒得栩栩如生；同时，整幅画面传达出的欢乐情绪简直要溢出纸面，可以说是百分之百地忠于原型了。
t_en: The frantic brushwork combines with bold colors to vividly bring the character to life, while the joyful emotion conveyed by the whole portrait spills out from the canvas. You can say it's a hundred percent faithful in its likeness to the original.

t_ch: 你决定将它从墙上揭下。这样的杰作不能公诸于世——至少不能被她看到。
t_en: You decide to peel it off the wall. Such a masterpiece can scarcely be made public to the world — in any case, not to her at least.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 此处贴着一幅艳美绝俗的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的男性神情真挚，口衔红花，热烈如炽焰。
t_en: There's an otherworldly, sensuous portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The guy in the poster appears sincere, gripping a rose in his teeth as he blazes with enthusiasm.

t_ch: 你明白，这就是那种曾风靡一时的某类作品中常见的男一号：永远热情似火，永远主动出击，永远毫不遮掩地散发自己的魅力。我们通常称之为…芳心纵火犯。
t_en: You identify him as the classic male lead in a once-popular period genre: Always fiery, always aggressive, always unabashedly charismatic. We usually call this kind of guy... a reckless Lothario.

t_ch: 你决定将它从墙上揭下。毕竟你知道画中人物的原型是谁——你明白，爱是要分对象的。
t_en: You decide to peel it off the wall. After all, you know who it's a portrait of — and love must always have an object of its desires.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 此处贴着一幅艳美绝俗的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的男性神情淡漠，手执蓝花，冷艳如冰霜。
t_en: There's an otherworldly, sensuous portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The guy in the poster appears aloof, holding a blue flower as cool as ice.

t_ch: 你明白，这就是那种曾风靡一时的某类作品中常见的男二号：永远寡淡如水，永远保持距离，永远不遗余力地克制自己的感情。我们通常称之为……
t_en: You identify him as the classic second male lead in a once-popular period genre: Always indifferent, always at a distance, always struggling to keep emotions in check. We usually call this kind of guy...

t_ch: …可恶，你本来想给他取个更有格调的外号。但你的脑子已经被「冷面小青龙」几个字占满了。
t_en: ...Darn it — you wanted to come up with a cooler nickname. But those kilobytes of brainpower seem preoccupied with an existing name: Cold Dragon Young.

t_ch: 你决定将它从墙上揭下。毕竟你们无名客做好事向来不留名，更遑论如此精致的画像了。
t_en: You decide to peel it off the wall. The Nameless have never left behind any credit for good deeds done, let alone for a portrait as exquisite as this.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 此处贴着一幅艳美绝俗的画像…或者说，通缉令。画中的女性笑靥如花，手捧…呃，相机？含情脉脉地望着你，或者你身后的某处。
t_en: There's an otherworldly, sensuous portrait here... or rather, a Wanted poster. The woman in the poster is smiling like a flower, holding a... camera? She looks at you affectionately — or possibly at somewhere just behind you.

t_ch: 老实说，你很难认真看待眼前这精美的人像。它与她是那么相似，却又在各种意义上都很不同——非要说的话，或许是少了一些欢乐…？
t_en: Honestly, it's hard to take seriously this beautiful portrait before you. It looks exactly like her, yet different in every sense — if you twisted my arm, perhaps it's a little less joyful...?

t_ch: 你决定将它从墙上揭下。如果她喜欢，再把这通缉令贴回来也不迟。
t_en: You decide to peel it off the wall. If she likes it, you can stick it back up.

t_ch: 你能看看这些通缉令吗？
t_en: Can you take a look at these wanted posters?

t_ch: 嗯？什么通缉令？
t_en: Mmm? Which Wanted poster?

t_ch: …啊，你竟然把它们全部集齐了。我本来安排了铁卫把它们揭掉，但看来还是你的动作比较快。
t_en: ...Ah, you got them all together. I asked guards to help me rip them down, but I see that you're far quicker.

t_ch: 你这样也算是帮了我们一个忙。请稍等，我去准备给你的报酬……
t_en: You've done me a huge favor. Hang on a moment. Let me prepare a reward for you...

t_ch: ——你先等会儿，我有话要问！
t_en: — Hang on. I've something to ask you!

t_ch: 你说吧。
t_en: Speak.

t_ch: 这些…狂野的画像是谁画的？
t_en: Who painted these... wild portraits?

t_ch: 狂野？你是在说我画的这些吗？
t_en: Wild? You mean these paintings I did?

t_ch: 感觉…也没那么狂野吧。可你和佩拉都这么说，难道确实是我画得不好看……
t_en: It feels... not that wild at all. But both you and Pela have said that. Am I really a bad painter...

t_ch: 没有，你画得很传神！
t_en: No no, your painting is very vivid!

t_ch: 「传神」…通缉令要的就是这个。能受到你这样高度表扬，我感到很荣幸。谢谢你。
t_en: "Vivid"... That's exactly what wanted posters called for. It's an honor to receive such high praise like this from you. Thank you so much.

t_ch: 你看，这幅——我记得你这位同伴是叫「三月七」吧？这张画像是我最满意的…我觉得它非常生动。
t_en: Look, this one — I remembered your companion's name is March 7th, right? This is the one I'm proudest of... I think it's indeed very vivid.

t_ch: 没天分就不要学人画画了…
t_en: If you don't have the natural talent for it, don't learn how to paint...

t_ch: …你说得对。画画这方面，我还得多向佩拉请教才是。
t_en: ...You're right. When it comes to painting, I've still got so much to learn from Pela.

t_ch: 可惜当时情况紧急，画得实在太仓促，否则我还能再打磨一下……
t_en: It's a shame that things were so urgent. I had to paint this one in a hurry — otherwise I would've polished it a lot more...

t_ch: 这些…美型的画像是谁画的？
t_en: Who painted these... magnificent portraits?

t_ch: 看这华丽的风格…大概是佩拉的作品。原来她还自己重新画了一版啊…这件事我确实不知情。
t_en: This gorgeous style... I presume it's Pela's work? So she even painted one version of it herself... I never knew about that.

t_ch: 我只听说她很会画画，还因此有不少拥趸，其他的便不清楚了。如果你实在好奇，可以亲自去问问她相关细节。
t_en: All I've heard is that she's a wonderful painter with lots of fans because of it. That's all I know. If you're really curious, you can ask her about the rest in person.

t_ch: 为什么要通缉我们？
t_en: Why are we on this Wanted list?

t_ch: 你忘了吗？当时可可利亚大人称你们意图颠覆贝洛伯格，下令全军通缉你们。情况紧急，我便亲自画了一版通缉令，让部下张贴在各处。
t_en: Have you forgotten? Madam Cocolia said you lot were trying to overthrow Belobog, and put out a Wanted order on your heads. It was urgent, so I painted a Wanted poster and got my staff to post them all over the place.

t_ch: 不过，你们已经证明了自己的清白，通缉一事也就到此为止了。先前给你们添了不少麻烦…还得再向你们说声抱歉。
t_en: But you've already proved your innocence, and so that's the end of this Wanted matter. I'm sorry for causing you so much trouble before... I owe you another apology.

t_ch: 你能看看这些通缉令吗？
t_en: Can you take a look at these wanted posters?

t_ch: 通缉令？那是什么？
t_en: Wanted posters? What are those for?

t_ch: …这些居然还没撤掉吗？！杰帕…啊不，戍卫官他到底在干什么……
t_en: ...Haven't these been removed yet!? Gepa— Ack, I mean, what the heck is the captain up to...

t_ch: 啊，我、我…我不认识这些东西，哈哈。什么通缉令？听都没听过。你快拿走吧，想收藏还是想干嘛都随你，再见。
t_en: Ah, I—I... I've never seen these before in my life, haha. What Wanted posters? Never heard of 'em. You can take those away. Keep them or do whatever you want. Goodbye.

t_ch: ——你先等会，我有话要问！
t_en: ...Hang on. I've something to ask you!
...Ugh.

t_ch: 这些…狂野的画像是谁画的？
t_en: Who painted these... wild portraits?

t_ch: 是杰帕德长官画的。
t_en: Captain Gepard did.

t_ch: 不许问其他那些是谁画的。我不知道。你快走吧，我还有事，再见。
t_en: Don't ask me who painted the rest. I don't know. Hurry up and move along, I'm busy. Goodbye.

t_ch: 这些…美型的画像是谁画的？
t_en: Who painted these... magnificent portraits?

t_ch: 我不知道。画得真好看啊，会是谁呢？好想认识一下哦。
t_en: I don't know. They're so beautiful. Who could it be? I'd love to know.

t_ch: 真不知道？
t_en: You really have no idea?

t_ch: 当然啦，我怎么可能知道啊……
t_en: Of course, how would I...

t_ch: 不会是你画的吧！
t_en: Are you sure it's not... YOU!?

t_ch: 你怎么知道的？！
t_en: How'd you know!?

t_ch: …不是！我刚刚什么都没说……
t_en: ...No! I said nothing!

t_ch: 好吧，我不问了。
t_en: Alright, I'm not asking anyway.

t_ch: 好，祝你度过愉快的一天，我先走了……
t_en: Well, have a nice day then. I'm off...

t_ch: …你别再拿那种眼神看我了！我招了，我全都招……
t_en: ...Don't look at me like that! I confess, I confess to everything...

t_ch: 这些…通缉令…就是我画的啦！这下你满意了吗！
t_en: These... Wanted posters... I painted them! Are you happy?

t_ch: 当然满意，画得很好看啊。
t_en: Of course I am. They're nice.

t_ch: 求求你别再夸了…我要受不了了……
t_en: Please stop it... I can't take any more teasing...

t_ch: 不满意！怎么没给我也画一个？
t_en: No way! Why haven't you painted one of me?

t_ch: 你、你不要得寸进尺！
t_en: You—you're asking too much!

t_ch: 没画你是因为…我忘了你长什么样了……
t_en: I never painted you because... because I forgot what you looked like...

t_ch: …对不起！
t_en: ...Sorry!

t_ch: 那个…我都告诉你这么多了，你可千万别跟其他人说啊！这件事你知我知就够了…拜托你了！
t_en: Well... I've already said far too much. Please don't tell anyone else! Only us two can know about this... please!

t_ch: 为什么要通缉我们？
t_en: Why are we on this Wanted list?

t_ch: 因为你们意图颠覆贝洛伯格啊，忘了？
t_en: Because you tried to overthrow Belobog, remember?

t_ch: …开个玩笑，我知道你们是清白的。不过，在那之后，杰帕德长官应该已经安排人把通缉令都撤掉了啊——这事还是我当面向他申请的呢。
t_en: ...Just kidding, I know you're innocent. But after all of that, Captain Gepard should've had your arrest warrants canceled by now — I submitted an application for it in person.

t_ch: 总之…我之后会再去向他确认下状况。要是还有没回收的通缉令就糟了……
t_en: Anyway... I'll check in with him later to confirm everything's okay. It'd be terrible if there were any outstanding warrants that weren't revoked...

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 看来这就是最后一张通缉令了。贝洛伯格的市容市貌因你焕然一新，恭喜你！
t_en: This looks like the last one. Belobog's cityscape has been completely revitalized thanks to you. Congratulations!

t_ch: 清点过后，你发现这通缉令统共七张——不过很可惜，即使你费力将这七章画像集齐，也没有被封印的大守护者突然现身，声称能够满足你的任何愿望。
t_en: You count and find seven Wanted posters in total — but unfortunately, despite collecting all seven of these, no Supreme Guardian pops out of nowhere to grant fulfillment of your wishes.

t_ch: 有关通缉令的一切到这里就全部结束了。不过，如果你的好奇心足够旺盛，也不妨去找你的铁卫朋友——
t_en: That's all there is regarding these Wanted posters. But if you're still curious, you might be able to hit up some of your Silvermane Guard friends —

t_ch: 杰帕德与佩拉——你可以问问他们，这些通缉令为何个个千奇百怪。
t_en: Gepard and Pela — you can ask them why these Wanted posters are so strange.

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 在你眼前的是一个近乎等身的球型仪器。从它布满灰尘的状态可以看出，已经很久没有人用手转动过它了。
t_en: In front of you is a spherical device that is almost as tall as a person. The amount of dust on it suggests it has not been used for a while.

t_ch: 「贝洛伯格人都不在乎地理吗？」——这样的疑惑跳入你的脑中。但你仔细回想了一下这颗星球的境遇，发现人们对「地理」这门学科兴致寡淡倒也情有可原。
t_en: "Don't Belobogians care about geography?" This question crosses your mind. However, given what this planet has been through, it's not all that surprising that people here have little to no interest in geography.

t_ch: 对贝洛伯格人来说，星球仪只是一件古董玩物。和一张详细标记出所有商铺位置的行政区地图相比，它毫无实用价值。
t_en: To Belobogians, this armillary sphere is but an antique plaything. It has no real practical use, especially when compared with a map of the Administrative District that explicitly depicts the locations of all shops.

t_ch: 转动星球仪，复兴地理学！
t_en: Spin the globe. Revive geography!

t_ch: 你情绪高涨，信心满满。动手吧，让地理学再次伟大！
t_en: You now feel energized and confident. Give it a go and make geography great again!

t_ch: 太棒了！我最讨厌的就是地理。
t_en: Ugh, great. I hate geography the most.

t_ch: 你止住了向星球仪啐上一口的冲动。不文明是其次——主要原因是布洛妮娅就站在你的身后。
t_en: You resist the urge to spit on the armillary sphere not just because that'd be rude. Mostly it's because Bronya is standing right behind you.

t_ch: 找到贝洛伯格的位置。
t_en: Find the location of Belobog.

t_ch: 你花了一段时间在球型的表面仔细搜索，终于找到了贝洛伯格的位置。
t_en: You eventually pinpoint Belobog's location on the globe's surface after diligent inspection.

t_ch: 当然，这个古董星球仪上没有直接标识出「贝洛伯格」几个大字——你之所以能找到它的确切位置，全凭此前在列车上看到的雅利洛-Ⅵ全景资料。
t_en: Of course, "Belobog" is not inscribed anywhere on the surface of this antique armillary sphere. The only reason you can pinpoint its exact location is that you've once seen Jarilo-VI's panoramic view on the Express.

t_ch: 四处瞎看。
t_en: Blindly look around.

t_ch: 你的脑中毫无规划，一片混乱。那些杂乱无章的地名被你的视觉神经吸收，下一秒又被干干净净地抛在脑后。
t_en: You looked around aimlessly, and all your thoughts are completely muddled together. All those random place names are reflected onto your visual receptors but are immediately discarded by your brain the very next second.

t_ch: 可惜，虽然你的志向远大，但地理就是一门死记硬背的学问。你必须找到一个记忆的基准点，一个突破口……
t_en: Despite your great aspirations, geography is a field that relies on memorization. To make a breakthrough, you will have to find an efficient mnemonic.

t_ch: 找到贝洛伯格的位置。
t_en: Find the location of Belobog.

t_ch: 你花了一段时间在球型的表面仔细搜索，终于找到了贝洛伯格的位置。
t_en: You eventually pinpoint Belobog's location on the globe's surface after much effort.

t_ch: 当然，这个古董星球仪上没有直接标识出「贝洛伯格」几个大字——你之所以能找到它的确切位置，全凭此前在列车上看到的雅利洛-Ⅵ全景资料。
t_en: Of course, "Belobog" is not inscribed anywhere on the surface of this antique armillary sphere. The only reason you can pinpoint its exact location is that you've once seen Jarilo-VI's panoramic view on the Express.

t_ch: 很好，现在你找到一个靠谱的基准点了。下一步该怎么做？集中思维，控制视线；拨开迷雾，探寻知识。
t_en: Now that you finally have a starting point, what should be your next move? Concentrate your thoughts, fine-tune your vision, evict all distractions, and seek truth with your own eyes.

t_ch: 东、南、西、北——你总要先定下一个「开拓」的方向。
t_en: North, east, south, and west. You must first decide on the next trailblazing direction.

t_ch: 看向东方。
t_en: Look to the east.

t_ch: 你看向东方。那是一片广袤的内陆平原，被错综复杂的边境线划分成数十个区块。每个区块可能代表着一个城邦，甚至是一个国家。
t_en: You look to the east and see a vast inland plain that is partitioned into dozens of subdivisions by a labyrinthine web of borders. Each subdivision could be a city or even a whole country.

t_ch: 你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你仿佛看到了鳞次栉比的地髓工厂，来自各个世界的商人、游客在城市的街巷间游荡，诉说着令人啧啧称奇的旅行故事。
t_en: Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you see orderly lined arrays of Geomarrow factories, merchants coming from various worlds, and tourists lingering in the city streets while sharing fascinating tales about their travels.

t_ch: 下一步该去哪儿？
t_en: Where to go next?

t_ch: 继续向东。
t_en: Go further east.

t_ch: 内陆平原的最东边零散分布着几个小城镇，它们的名字都无比拗口。除此以外，你没有发现什么有趣的东西。
t_en: Situated at the farthest east of the inland plain are some tiny settlements with names that are hard to pronounce. Other than this, you fail to discover anything noteworthy.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 东北方有什么？
t_en: What is in the northeast?

t_ch: 内陆的东北方向是一片空白之地。或许那里土地荒芜，不宜居住？无论如何，有关它的秘密都已经被历史埋葬。
t_en: What can be found in the northeast are nothing but barren lands. Perhaps those lands are just too desolate to be habitable? Whatever the case might be, its secrets remain deeply buried in history.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 东南方有什么？
t_en: What is in the southeast?

t_ch: 内陆平原的东南方是一个被峡谷隔开的孤单国度，它的名字已经难以看清。灾难降临时，那里的人们一定绝望而无助。
t_en: Laying in the southeast of the inland plain is a deserted realm isolated by canyons. The name is no longer legible. When the calamity fell, its people must have been devastated.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 把视线带回贝洛伯格。
t_en: Now back to Belobog.

t_ch: 看向西方。
t_en: Look to the west.

t_ch: 你看向西方。那里是无垠的大海，尚未被寒潮永久冰封。
t_en: You look to the west and see a great ocean that is yet to be permanently encased in ice by the Eternal Freeze.

t_ch: 你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你好像看到了浩瀚的蓝洋，造型各异的船只舰艇浮游在粼粼波光之上。
t_en: Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you seem to catch sight of a blue ocean with vessels of every shape sailing on its dazzling waves.

t_ch: 继续向西。
t_en: Go further west.

t_ch: 西之西，无穷无尽的海洋。不知道这个世界的原住民用了多长时间才发现自己的家园是一颗球体。
t_en: The furthest west is an endless ocean. It's not known how long it took the inhabitants of this world to discover that their home is situated on a globe.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 西北方有什么？
t_en: What's in the northwest?

t_ch: 海洋上零星散布着几个没有被命名的小岛。也许这个世界也曾有流浪汉留下孤岛求生的日记？
t_en: Scattered in the ocean are a few unnamed isles. Perhaps there are journals left behind by drifters documenting their survival on those isolated islands?

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 西南方有什么？
t_en: What's in the southwest?

t_ch: 往海洋的西南方看去，你发现一片巨大的黑色圆形。它一度象征着死亡和恐怖，漩涡会无情地吞没一切船只和生命。令人着迷。
t_en: You look to the southwest of the ocean and spot a colossal black circle. It once represented death and terror, a vortex engulfing all ships and lives. Truly fascinating.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 把视线带回贝洛伯格。
t_en: Now back to Belobog.

t_ch: 看向南方。
t_en: Look to the south.

t_ch: 你看向南方。那是一片巨大的盆地，整个区块被繁多的森林、湖泊和湿地点缀。
t_en: You look to the south and see a giant basin, where the whole region is decorated by many forests, lakes, and wetlands.

t_ch: 你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你好像看到了自然焕发的盎然生机，形态各异的动物在天空、陆地和水中迸发着蓬勃的生命力。
t_en: Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you seem to witness nature's utmost exuberance, with animals of every shape thriving in the air, on the land, and in the water.

t_ch: 继续向南。
t_en: Go further south.

t_ch: 一片淡绿色的湖泊，那里的气候一定十分温暖，并且栖息着很多奇特的生物——比如翼展两米的蜻蜓，数十吨重的巨鳄，还有……
t_en: A greenish lake with a warm climate. Many marvelous species choose to reside here, from dragonflies with wings that span six feet to giant crocodiles that weigh dozens of tons, and many besides...

t_ch: 呃，别想太多。或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Um. Don't make too much of it. Perhaps you should just change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 西南方有什么？
t_en: What's in the southwest?

t_ch: 一大片森林——大到难以置信，大到几乎占有和海洋近似的面积。肯定有不少反物质军团的兵卒在那里迷了路。
t_en: It's a forest so unbelievably large that it could almost match the size of an ocean. There must've been a great many soldiers of the Antimatter Legion who got lost in it.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 东南方有什么？
t_en: What is in the southeast?

t_ch: 沙漠…紧邻着森林和湖区，真是神奇的地理地貌。沙漠里也散布着几座带有名字的城市，养活它们的可能是地下丰富的地髓资源促进的贸易。
t_en: A desert very close to lakes and forests... What a peculiar landscape. There are a few named cities scattered in the desert. What sustains them must've been the trade fueled by the rich Geomarrow resources found underground.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 把视线带回贝洛伯格。
t_en: Now back to Belobog.

t_ch: 看向北方。
t_en: Look to the north.

t_ch: 你看向北方。那是一大片耸立的山脉，一直蔓延向世界的尽头。
t_en: You look to the north and see stunningly high mountain ranges that extend all the way to the world's end.

t_ch: 你的右脑开始飞速运转，形成一幅图像。你仿佛看到了高耸的山峰插入云霄，从尚未被白雪覆盖的山崖向远处眺望，好一番恢弘壮阔的景象。
t_en: Your right cerebral hemisphere is now running at full speed, conjuring up a vision where you seem to catch a glimpse of cloud-piercing summits and a breathtaking view on top of a mountain ridge that is yet to be covered by snow.

t_ch: 继续向北。
t_en: Go further north.

t_ch: 北岳之北，是一座名叫查拉图斯特拉萨帕拉——你放弃了读出那个名字——的山峰。毋庸说，那应该就是雅利洛-Ⅵ的最高峰。
t_en: North of the northern mountains is a summit called Zarathustrazapala, the name of which you've already given up trying to pronounce. That is indubitably Jarilo-VI's highest point.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 西北方有什么？
t_en: What's in the northwest?

t_ch: 西北方…慢着，这是……
t_en: Northwest... Wait a minute, this is...

t_ch: 你的视线停在一片被群山包围的地块上。它或许是平平无奇的洼地，又或许是什么雅利洛-Ⅵ独有的奇特地貌…但对你来说，它究竟是什么并不重要。
t_en: You set eyes upon a land surrounded by mountain ranges. It might just be a typical marshland, or perhaps some peculiar landscape exclusive to Jarilo-VI... But that makes little to no difference to you either way.

t_ch: 唯一重要的一点是：那片地块在星球仪上呈现出来的形状，简直和列车长帕姆一模一样！
t_en: Perhaps the only meaningful aspect is how that piece of land on the armillary sphere is shaped exactly like Conductor Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 你不禁展开了联想——也许「开拓」阿基维利上次来到这颗星球时过于流连忘返，于是在临走前给雅利洛-Ⅵ盖下了印章……
t_en: You can't help but think it might be a commemorative stamp left by Akivili the Trailblaze when he last paid a visit to this planet...

t_ch: 真是个奇妙的发现。看吧，枯燥如地理学也偶有其美妙之处。
t_en: What a fascinating discovery. Look, there are jewels of wonder even in a seemingly dull discipline like geography.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 东北方有什么？
t_en: What is in the northeast?

t_ch: 群山，群山，还是群山。你几乎看到了军团来犯之后，无助的灾民躲入山间，等待末日来临的凄惨景象。
t_en: Mountains, mountains, and even more mountains. You can almost see how miserable it must've been for all those refugees who fled into the mountains, seeking shelter after the Legion's attack while helplessly waiting for calamity's arrival.

t_ch: 或许你该换个方向，再探索一次。
t_en: Perhaps you should change direction and explore again.

t_ch: 把视线带回贝洛伯格。
t_en: Now back to Belobog.

t_ch: 结束探索。
t_en: End exploration.

t_ch: 你结束了这段穿越千年的地理之旅。星球仪静静地停在你面前，原本布满灰尘的表面多出了几个显眼的巴掌印。
t_en: You end this geographical journey spanning millennia. The armillary sphere comes to a quiet stop in front of you. There are now a few discernible handprints left on its previously dusty surface.

t_ch: 现在，考试时间到——
t_en: Now, it's time for an exam.

t_ch: ——开玩笑的，没有人不痛恨应试教育。就让和古代雅利洛-Ⅵ有关的地理知识停留在兴趣的范畴吧。
t_en: Just kidding. Everyone despises those good-for-nothing exams. Let's just keep the study of Jarilo-VI's ancient geography confined within the realm of extracurricular pursuits.

t_ch: 过去的荣誉，已经无法评判我现在的成就，区区第一真狸大学的一等荣誉学位，在我看来已经不值一提。
t_en: My past honors are no longer a measure of my current achievements. A mere first-class honors degree from University of Furberitas Prime is, in my opinion, hardly worth mentioning.

t_ch: 既然来了二相乐园，这回我真狸医生要去绘世学院进修人生中的第九个博士学位。
t_en: Now that I'm in Planarcadia, I, Dr. Furatio, shall be heading to Graphia Academy to pursue my ninth doctorate.

t_ch: 拉帝奥教授，有人冒充你。
t_en: Professor Ratio, someone's impersonating you.

t_ch: 习惯了，经常有人冒充我的名字发表一些庸俗、愚钝、可笑至极的观点…不对，你什么意思哩？
t_en: I'm used to it. People are always using my name to publish vulgar, dull, and utterly ridiculous opinions... Wait, what do you mean by that?

t_ch: 绘世学院发博士学位吗？
t_en: Does Graphia Academy issue PhDs?

t_ch: 绘世学院，那不是直接发院士学位吗？
t_en: Graphia Academy? Don't they just give you the academician title on the spot?

t_ch: 什、什么！那好吧，我对学术已经厌倦哩！这回我要读的是一个博升专学位——算了，编不下去哩！
t_en: Wh—What? Fine, then! I've grown tired of academia anyway! This time, I shall pursue a post-doctoral degree... Ugh, who am I kidding... I can't keep this up!

t_ch: 太好狸！太好狸！
t_en: Fantastiki! Fantastiki!

t_ch: 天才的头脑需要辅之以必要的艺术修养，俗话说，不会画画的物理学家不是好的数学老师。另外，注意你言语里的错别字…不对，你是不是发现什么哩？
t_en: A brilliant mind must be complemented by the necessary artistic refinement. As they say, a physicist who cannot paint will not make a good mathematics teacher. Also, "fantastic" spells with a "c"... Wait a minute, did you do that on purpose?

t_ch: 嘶…你是什么时候看出我不是本尊的？不管怎么说，千变万化本来就是狸狸的生存之道，狸狸的事你不要多管！
t_en: Hmph... When did you realize I wasn't him? Regardless, constant transformation is the furbo way of life. You stay out of my business!

t_ch: 狸狸错了…敢问大人何方神圣？
t_en: Sorry about that... May I inquire who I'm speaking with?

t_ch: 银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm the Galactic Baseballer.

t_ch: 银河球棒侠？这个名字是不是在哪里……
t_en: Galactic Baseballer? Where have I heard that name before?

t_ch: …啊！您、您难道就是我们《狸狸周刊》传说中的新老板？
t_en: Ah! A—Are you the new owner of our Furbobo Weekly?

t_ch: 真理医生门下大弟子。
t_en: Dr. Ratio's senior disciple.

t_ch: 我不信。这肯定是你自封的！到底…你到底是什么来头？
t_en: I don't buy it. You must have given yourself that title! Just who... who in the world are you?

t_ch: 既然如此，看我的工牌…
t_en: In that case, check out my name card...

t_ch: 狸猫杂志社的新老板！
t_en: The new boss of furbo Magazines!

t_ch: 啊？哈哈哈哈，大人您真会开玩……
t_en: Huh? Hahaha, you really know your way of joking around...

t_ch: …等等，真的是吗？
t_en: ...Wait, are you for real?

t_ch: 狸猫杂志社的新老板。
t_en: The new boss of furbo Magazines.

t_ch: 啊？哈哈哈哈，大人您真会开玩……
t_en: Huh? Hahaha, you really know your way of joking around...

t_ch: …等等，真的是吗？
t_en: ...Wait, are you for real?

t_ch: 没错。你被开除了！
t_en: That's right. You're fired!

t_ch: 啊啊啊，不要哇，在下只是一时糊涂，变作拉帝奥教授的模样，想享受几句对智者的赞美哩，在下真的错了啊啊啊……
t_en: Aaaaah, please nooo! This humble one merely had a momentary lapse in judgment, taking on Professor Ratio's form just to bask in a few words of praise meant for the wise! This humble one is truly sorry, aaaaaah...

t_ch: 慈悲为怀，拿捏狸心。
t_en: I'll be merciful, if only to gain furbo allegiance.

t_ch: 在下再也不敢了，在下保证以后永远不动歪脑筋……在下这就去正经工作，老板再见！
t_en: I wouldn't dare do it again, I promise I'll never have any funny ideas... I'll go do some honest work right now, farewell boss!

t_ch: 果断开除，雷厉风行。
t_en: Fire decisively on the stop.

t_ch: 哼，小气的老板！再见——再也不见！
t_en: Hmph, what a petty boss! Goodbye, and good riddance!

t_ch: 在你眼前的宽阔的办公桌属于这个星球的最高统治者——贝洛伯格的大守护者。它看起来尊贵、典雅，被打理得井井有条，与它的主人如出一辙。
t_en: The wide desk before you belongs to the majestic ruler of this planet — the Supreme Guardian of Belobog. It looks distinguished, elegant, and well-kept — just like its owner.

t_ch: 当看到这样一张办公桌时，不同的人会有不同反应：热爱秩序的人视之为完美的艺术品，啧啧称奇后便转身离去……
t_en: Different people may have different reactions when faced with such a desk: Those who love order view it as a perfect masterpiece, and leave filled with admiration...

t_ch: …而钟情于混乱的人则开始摩拳擦掌、试图让它回归宇宙无尽熵增的铁律中，并很快给自己找好了由头——「翻箱倒柜是勇者的特权」。
t_en: ...While those who love chaos lick their lips as they try to return things to the lawless entropy of the universe, quickly finding reason enough for themselves: "The valiant reserve the right to rummage through this desk."

t_ch: 那么，你认为自己是哪一种人呢？
t_en: So then, which kind of person are you?

t_ch: 我就是混乱的化身！
t_en: I am chaos incarnate!

t_ch: 自从被那位负创之神瞥视过后，你的内心深处就常有这样的念头了。这种想法本身无可厚非——只是，雅利洛-Ⅵ已成型的道德体系暂时不大认可这种行为。
t_en: You've felt this way ever since you were first glimpsed by the Blemished One. It's an understandable idea — it's just that this morality system of Jarilo-VI doesn't approve of this kind of behavior.

t_ch: 换言之，想在大守护者的办公桌上大闹一番是要付出代价的……
t_en: In other words, there's a price to pay for causing trouble at the Supreme Guardian's desk...

t_ch: 好在你在旅途中一路积善行德，曾收获不少来自道德层面的赞许。这点小小的缺德事怎会让你困扰？显然不会。
t_en: Luckily, you've built up a record of good deeds and praise along the way. Surely this little slip-up won't bother you? Obviously not.

t_ch: 退一万步讲，这里搞不好藏着珍贵的道具呢！你可千万不能错过——成功将自己说服，你便开始了自己的「工作」……
t_en: Take one big step back here — there must be something precious hidden here! You can't miss out on whatever it is. Having convinced yourself, you begin with your "work"...

t_ch: 想想看，当别人知道这件事后，会如何评价你和一众开拓客？——想到这里，你便觉得实在良心难安，于是悻悻然放弃了搞破坏的想法。
t_en: Think about it: What will people think of you and the Trailblazers once they find out about this? This thought leaves you feeling deeply uneasy, and so you give up this dastardly plan of yours.

t_ch: 我当然热爱秩序。
t_en: Of course, I love order.

t_ch: 你拒绝释放内心深处那无处发泄的破坏欲，只给办公桌留下了一个潇洒的背影。
t_en: You refuse to unleash your pent-up destructive desires, instead turning away to show your best side.

t_ch: 看看这里有些什么东西…
t_en: Let's see what's in here then...

t_ch: 首先是一盆可爱的花卉造景。你问为什么是造景？这是因为你看它常年摆放在此处，始终未有凋落的迹象…就像一朵真的假花。
t_en: First up is a cute flowerpot scene here. Why is it a scene? Because you've seen this year-round, and it's never wilted of lost leaves before... Just like a fake plant.

t_ch: 为了确认这一想法究竟是否正确，你试着掐了掐那柔嫩的花瓣——
t_en: To confirm the veracity of this hunch, you try to pinch one of the tender petals...

t_ch: 啊哦，出汁了。好吧，它不是造景。对不起。
t_en: Oh, wow — some sap. Alright, it's not fake. I'm sorry.

t_ch: 再看看有没有其他好玩的…
t_en: Let's see if there's anything else fun...

t_ch: 桌上放着几册装帧精美的书籍。你趁没人注意，便将其翻开随意浏览了起来——
t_en: Several beautifully bound books are on the desk. When nobody's looking, you crack one of them and browse through at random...

t_ch: 空的、空的、还是空的。难道这些书籍只是为了显示书桌主人高贵教养的装饰品吗？
t_en: Empty, empty, and still empty. Could these books merely be ornaments to show off the classy upbringing of their owner?

t_ch: 你还以为这里会有克里珀堡的机密文件呢。真没劲。至少得给本阅读物吧？
t_en: You really thought there'll be some top-secret documents on Qlipoth Fort here. How boring. At least give me something to read?

t_ch: 那就让我看看柜子里有什么…
t_en: Then let's take a look at what's in the closet...

t_ch: 柜子里肯定有东西——或者说，必须得有。不然你费尽心思翻箱倒柜是为了什么？总不可能真是为了制造破坏吧？
t_en: There's definitely something in the closet — or rather, there must be. Otherwise, why are you spending so much energy rummaging through these closets? It can't be to cause actual damage, right?

t_ch: 于是，你将手伸向了那仿佛掩藏了贝洛伯格一切秘密的柜门……
t_en: So, you reach out to the handle of the closet that seemingly contains every single secret in all of Belobog...

t_ch: 呃，{NICKNAME}，请你停手吧…这里真没东西，我保证。
t_en: Uh, {NICKNAME}, please stop that... There's nothing here, I promise you.

t_ch: 你直起腰，与布洛妮娅四目相对。
t_en: You lift your head and stare straight into Bronya's eyes.

t_ch: 这下糟了——她一定是早就发现你在这里鬼鬼祟祟，出于情面才没有立即制止你。怪不得你总觉得附近有道凌厉的目光呢……
t_en: This is bad — she must have found out you were snooping around here ages ago, but didn't stop you because she cared. No wonder you always feel as though a burning gaze is affixed upon you...

t_ch: 今天的事，我就睁一只眼闭一只眼…但希望你下次不要这样了。
t_en: I'll turn a blind eye today... but I hope that you won't do this next time.

t_ch: 我…我不是故意的。
t_en: It... it was an accident.

t_ch: 你放心，情况没有你想象得那么夸张。
t_en: Don't worry. It's not as bad as you think.

t_ch: 还是惩罚我吧，布洛妮娅大人！
t_en: Punish me, Lady Bronya!

t_ch: …你怎么这么积极啊？放心，情况没有你想象得那么夸张。
t_en: ...No need to make it weird. Don't worry. It's not as bad as you think.

t_ch: 真没东西？我不信。
t_en: Nothing? Really? I'm skeptical.

t_ch: 当然了，毕竟这书桌只是个装饰。
t_en: Of course, this desk is just for decoration.

t_ch: 其实这里只用作接待访客，我的工作场所另在别处。这是出于安全考虑——
t_en: This room's actually just where I receive guests, and my workspace is elsewhere. For the sake of security...

t_ch: 毕竟…想要翻大守护者办公桌的人可不止你一个呢。如果换作别人，现在恐怕已经被铁卫押走了吧。
t_en: After all... You're not the only one trying to rummage through the Supreme Guardian's desk. If it were anyone else, they'd be apprehended by the Silvermane Guards by now.

t_ch: 原来如此…对不起！
t_en: I understand... My apologies!

t_ch: 噗…别一本正经地道歉了，今天这事就当没发生过吧。
t_en: Pfft... Don't be so serious. Let's pretend that this never happened today.

t_ch: 可我什么也没得到，好亏！
t_en: But I got nothing, What a lousy bargain!

t_ch: 噗…这话倒是出乎我的意料。
t_en: Pfft... Now I didn't expect to hear that.

t_ch: 啊，对了。正好你在这儿——我有样东西要给你。
t_en: That's right. It's good that you're here — I've got something for you.

t_ch: 这是「科技护具」的配方，据说是寒潮之前的天外来客带来的技术…可惜，我们现在拥有的资源和技术还暂时无法将其复现。
t_en: This is the recipe for High-Tech Protective Gear, rumored to be brought in by visitors from beyond the sky before the Eternal Freeze... Unfortunately, with the resources and technology we have access to, we have yet to be able to reproduce it.

t_ch: 我想你在旅途中总会身陷险境，这护具或许能为你提供防护…你就尽管拿去用吧，别客气。
t_en: You're always in danger during your travels, and this protective gear might just protect you... Just use it. Don't be hesitant about it.

t_ch: 广场上的一眼喷泉，泉水凉爽而不寒冷。透过粼粼水波，你看见喷泉底部不规则地铺着一层金属钱币，想来这里也被当地人派上了许愿池的用场。
t_en: A fountain in the plaza is shooting out cool, tepid water. You can see through the ripples that the bottom of the fountain has metal coins strewn around. It looks like the locals use this as a kind of wishing pool.

t_ch: 数数有多少钱。
t_en: Count how much money there is.

t_ch: 你穷极无聊，决定数数喷泉里有多少钱——用无聊这个词，可能都是一种抬举——要知道喷泉里什么怪东西都有，硬币更是数不胜数。
t_en: You're so bored that you decide to count the money on the floor of the fountain — and "bored" is putting it lightly. There are loads of weird stuff floating down there, and there are more coins than can be counted.

t_ch: 你得用点更科学的办法才行。
t_en: You'll need a more scientific method.

t_ch: 硬着头皮数。
t_en: Force yourself to count them.

t_ch: 明白，最简单的办法往往最实用。泉水瀑落，反射的光线刺激着你的眼睛，直到所有东西在你看来都产生了三个重影……
t_en: You understand that the simplest solutions are often the most practical. As the waters fall, light refracts into your eyes until three superimposed images form before you...

t_ch: 五五计数法。
t_en: Count in fives.

t_ch: 你以五为单位，每数五个就停下来。不得不说，这一招最初卓有成效，但随着时间的推移，你的眼睛开始疲劳，记忆也不那么靠谱了……
t_en: You break things down into groups of five. This trick works great at first, but over time your eyes get tired and your memory grows unreliable...

t_ch: 科学计数法。
t_en: Use scientific notation.

t_ch: 并不是叫科学计数法的办法就很科学，你懂得这个道理，但还是决定给自己加点挑战。就这样，数字在你的脑中颠来倒去，妙谛无穷……
t_en: It's not that scientific notation is scientific per se. You understand this but still decide to give yourself a little challenge. Just like that, the numbers tumble through your brain with their limitless, profound meaning...

t_ch: 试试计算机图形学原理…？
t_en: Try utilizing principles of computer graphics...?

t_ch: 你眼睛一眨，已经将整个喷泉的模样化作高分辨率的图片保存在脑中；接着，你将这副图片在脑中二值化，利用形态学技术开始了疯狂运算……
t_en: In a flash, you transform the entire fountain into a hi-res image and save it in your memory. Next, you binarize these images in your mind, and use morphology tech to begin your crazy calculation...

t_ch: 最终，你还是数出了喷泉里的硬币数量：1{TEXTJOIN#44}{TEXTJOIN#45}。这个数字有意义吗？可能有。你把这个数字记了下来，但之后数字或许将是另一个数字了。
t_en: In the end, you reach the final count: 1{TEXTJOIN#44}{TEXTJOIN#45} coins. Is this number pertinent? Perhaps. You write it down, but then again the number might change later on.

t_ch: 投掷硬币许个愿吧。
t_en: Toss in a coin and make a wish.

t_ch: 你放弃了许愿。就这样吧，喷泉欢迎你之后再来拜访。
t_en: You've given up on wishes. That's it. You're welcome to visit the fountain again.

t_ch: 你摸出一枚本地硬币，掷入空中，同时心中默默许愿。愿望许完，硬币已经沉入泉水，成为无数反光物中平平无奇的一员了。
t_en: You pull out a local coin and make a wish as it flips through the air. Afterwards, the coin sinks down into the water, becoming just another unremarkable reflection among all the others.

t_ch: 有那么一会儿，什么都没有发生，你还以为自己受了欺骗。但——瞧！硬币落下就像第一块骨牌的失衡，引发了一系列连锁效应：有什么东西悠悠浮了上来，你赶紧抓住它。
t_en: For a while, nothing happens and you begin to feel deceived. But — look! The coin tumbles like the first domino, triggering a cascade of effects: Something floats on up, and you grab it.

t_ch: 你耐心等着愿望实现，却听见身后传来一声厉喝——原来这里禁止投币。你赶紧转身走开，也无暇思考愿望到底成真了没有。
t_en: You patiently wait for your wish to come true, when you hear a loud shout from behind — It turns out that you're not allowed to toss coins here. You hurriedly turn to walk away, with no time left to wonder if your wish came true.

t_ch: 你耐心等着愿望实现。长长的时间过去了，什么都没有发生，你得到的只有虚无。
t_en: You patiently wait for your wish to come true. A long time passes but nothing at all happens, and ends up with a fistful of nothingness.

t_ch: 打捞喷泉里的宝藏。
t_en: Fish the treasure out of the fountain.

t_ch: …等下，你认真的？这可是在广场上，大庭广众众目睽睽之下…退一万步说，就算没有人看见，它可是公共设施啊！这么做你的良心不会痛吗？
t_en: ...Hang on, are you serious? This is the plaza, in full view of the public... Take one big step back here: Even if nobody saw you, it's a public amenity! Wouldn't your conscience prevent you from doing this?

t_ch: 罢了罢了，看在这个想法十分具有创意的份上——尽管放手去做吧！只是…后果自负。
t_en: Forget it, seeing as this is such a creative idea — just go ahead and do it! But do so... at your own risk.

t_ch: 不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，和远古帝王的宝藏相比也毫不逊色！看！你居然从喷泉里找到了——固态净水！？在水池里找到这种东西，真是太合理了！
t_en: It begs saying that this fountain really does have everything in it, just like the ancient treasure tombs of the emperors gone by! Look! You somehow find amidst those ripples — solid water!? Makes sense to find it inside a wishing well, I suppose!

t_ch: 不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，和远古帝王的宝藏相比也毫不逊色！看！你居然从喷泉里找到了——吉姆•罗杰汽水？真是暴殄天物！
t_en: It begs to say that this fountain really does have everything in it, just like the ancient treasure tombs of emperors in the past! Look! You somehow find amidst those ripples — Jim Roger bread soda!? What a waste!

t_ch: 不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，和远古帝王的宝藏相比也毫不逊色！看！你居然从喷泉里找到了——熄灭原核？！难道反物质军团也在这里许过愿吗？
t_en: It begs to say that this fountain really does have everything in it, just like the ancient treasure tombs of the emperors gone by! Look! You somehow find amidst those ripples — Extinguished Core!? Has the Antimatter Legion ever made a wish here!?

t_ch: 你跳进喷泉，却发现今天的喷泉囊中羞涩，并没有什么有趣的东西。但你就这么湿漉漉地空手而归，总觉得有点吃亏。于是你顺手拿走了三块垃圾。
t_en: You jump into the fountain, only to find that there's nothing interesting there today. But you can't go back empty-handed and bedraggled like this, so you pick up three pieces of trash on your way out.

t_ch: 不得不说，这喷泉里真是什么都有，当然也包括硬币。你就是来干这个的——一枚脏兮兮的硬币——你最珍贵的宝藏！
t_en: It begs saying that this fountain really does have everything in it, including coins of course. That's what you're here for — one filthy coin, your most precious of treasures!

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 喷泉还是那个喷泉，或者说，许愿池。其中有价值的物件已被你一扫而空，只剩下水中层层叠叠的锈蚀硬币。
t_en: The fountain is still the same old fountain, or rather a wishing well. You've already swept away anything valuable in it, leaving behind only stacks of rusted coins.

t_ch: 你已经捞不到任何东西了。但你仍可以凭着兴趣数数它们到底有多少，或者投掷硬币许个愿——如果你还有硬币的话。
t_en: There's nothing left for you to get. But you can still count everything out of interest, or toss a coin in to make a wish — if you have any coins left, that is.

t_ch: 你放弃了许愿。就这样吧，喷泉欢迎你之后再来拜访。
t_en: You've given up on wishes. That's it. You're welcome to visit the fountain again.

t_ch: 你摸出一枚本地硬币，掷入空中，同时心中默默许愿。愿望许完，硬币已经沉入泉水，成为无数反光物中平平无奇的一员了。
t_en: You pull out a local coin and make a wish as it flips through the air. Afterwards, the coin sinks down into the water, becoming just another unremarkable reflection among all the others.

t_ch: 直觉告诉你，许愿池似乎已经无法为你提供更多东西了。
t_en: Intuition tells you that the fountain cannot provide you with anything else.

t_ch: 菲多拉的机器人。
t_en: Fidora's Robot.

t_ch: 谢天谢地，这什么逻辑语言中枢可算是卖出去了……
t_en: Thanks the heavens, this language module thing is finally sold...

t_ch: 我还以为这是没人要的垃圾哩。总之，多谢老板！
t_en: I just thought this is trash that nobody wanted. Anyway, thanks very much, boss!
